description: Central registry of all skills in the Skill System Standard

parallel_groups.description: Code review, testing strategy, and security review run concurrently during implementation phase
Telemetry collection and insight aggregation run asynchronously at pipeline.ended — non-blocking

skills.name: requirement-analyzer
skills.description: Extracts, normalizes, and validates requirements from raw input
skills.orchestration: MUST run first — entry point for all feature work

skills.name: architecture-design
skills.description: Produces system architecture from structured requirements; v1.3.0 adds domain_constraints input (from domain specialists) and integration patterns reference table in Step 4
skills.orchestration: MUST run second — depends on requirement-analyzer output

feedback_routes.description: Re-run requirement analysis when architecture reveals undefined actors or unstated preconditions

skills.name: feature-planning
skills.description: Breaks architecture into tasks with dependencies and roadmap. v2.0.0 adds opt-in companion task generation (REVIEW/TEST/VALIDATION/DOC per TASK). Backward compatible when companion_generation.enabled=false (default).
skills.orchestration: MUST run third — produces implementation plan

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture when planning finds tasks that cannot map to any module

skills.name: clean-code-review
skills.description: Analyzes code against SOLID and clean architecture principles
skills.orchestration: Runs in parallel with testing-strategy during implementation

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture when code review reveals module boundary violations or coupling issues
Re-run planning when reviewed code reveals tasks were scoped incorrectly

skills.name: testing-strategy
skills.description: Produces a complete, layered testing strategy — test pyramid, naming conventions, PBT candidates, contract test specs, parameterized test tables, test data strategy, test double taxonomy, flakiness rules, mutation score targets, and quality gates
skills.orchestration: Runs after feature-planning is approved; output passed as first-class input to test-generator v2.0.0 and mutation-test-generator

feedback_routes.description: Re-run requirement analysis when test planning reveals gaps in requirement coverage
Re-run planning when tasks are too vague to produce concrete test cases

skills.name: schema-validator
skills.description: Validates JSON output against a declared schema
skills.orchestration: Utility skill — invoked by orchestrator between pipeline steps

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Analyzes architecture and code for security vulnerabilities and threats
skills.orchestration: Runs after architecture-design and during/after clean-code-review

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture when security review detects structural vulnerabilities (e.g., missing auth layer, insecure data flow)
Re-run requirements when threat modeling reveals security requirements that were not specified

skills.name: deployment-strategy
skills.description: Defines deployment pipeline, environment promotion, rollback criteria
skills.orchestration: Runs after testing-strategy — final step before production

feedback_routes.description: Re-run testing when deployment strategy reveals that quality gates are insufficient for safe promotion

skills.name: documentation-generator
skills.description: Auto-generates documentation from requirements, architecture, and code analysis
skills.orchestration: Runs asynchronously after upstream skills complete — non-blocking

skills.name: orchestrator
skills.description: Meta-skill that executes the skill pipeline with routing, validation, and HITL gates
skills.orchestration: Meta-skill — invokes all other skills per pipeline definition

skills.name: doc-maintainer
skills.description: Autonomous documentation maintenance engine — detects changes, creates/updates .md files, ensures consistency
skills.orchestration: System maintenance skill — runs on change detection, not pipeline execution

skills.name: skill-authoring
skills.description: Meta-skill — designs, generates, validates, and governs all other skills
skills.orchestration: Meta-skill — invoked to create or govern any other skill in the system

skills.name: context-memory
skills.description: Manages session state schema, compression rules, and multi-turn pipeline resumption protocol
skills.orchestration: Utility protocol — invoked by orchestrator to serialize, compress, and restore session state

skills.name: observability
skills.description: Metrics collection, aggregation, threshold alerting, and health status reporting for the skill pipeline — defines the canonical per-skill metrics schema enforced by all skills
skills.orchestration: Utility protocol — invoked by orchestrator at 4 defined collection points (skill.started, skill.completed, gate event, pipeline.ended); individual skills only need to produce a compliant metrics object

skills.name: quality-scoring
skills.description: Scores a skill on 7 dimensions against the quality rubric to determine registration eligibility
skills.orchestration: Meta utility — invoked by skill-authoring (SKL-012) as part of the registration gate

skills.name: skill-lifecycle
skills.description: Governs skill progression through 12 lifecycle stages from intent discovery to retirement
skills.orchestration: Governance meta-skill — invoked to check or advance any skill's lifecycle stage

skills.name: trigger-engineering
skills.description: Defines and refines skill trigger conditions with positive, negative, and conflict-resolution rules
skills.orchestration: Meta utility — invoked during skill design to produce activation trigger specs

skills.name: validation-rules
skills.description: Enforces structural and content rules on skills/index.yaml entries before and after registration
skills.orchestration: Validation utility — invoked by skill-authoring during Step 8 (multi-layer validation)

skills.name: versioning
skills.description: Assigns semver version bumps for skill changes and enforces registry and changelog versioning policy
skills.orchestration: Governance utility — invoked during skill evolution or release to determine correct version bump

skills.name: create-sub-agent
skills.description: Compiles a capability requirement into a fully-specified orchestrator-controlled sub-agent spec with I/O contracts and tool permissions
skills.orchestration: Meta-skill — invoked to scaffold a new sub-agent module from a capability specification

skills.name: state-manager
skills.description: Reads, writes, diffs, snapshots, and restores shared system state — the sole write interface to cross-skill state
skills.orchestration: Infrastructure primitive — invoked by every skill that reads or writes shared state

skills.name: context-compressor
skills.description: Semantically compresses large context payloads to fit within token budget tiers without losing decision-critical information. v2.0.0 adds cascade compression (light → medium → aggressive), auto_compress mode, pipeline_state and session_history content types, and token_efficiency_score.
skills.orchestration: Invoked automatically (auto_compress=true) when context pressure exceeds the active token budget tier; otherwise called explicitly by any skill needing context reduction

feedback_routes.description: Alert orchestrator when content cannot be compressed below budget without critical data loss
Inform orchestrator when cascade escalated to aggressive level — significant context was dropped

skills.name: dependency-analyzer
skills.description: Builds and maintains a directed dependency graph; detects cycles; computes module ripple effects
skills.orchestration: Runs after every code.changed event and before any change-impact-analyzer invocation

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture design when a cross-module dependency cycle is detected

skills.name: change-impact-analyzer
skills.description: Computes full impact surface of a proposed change across modules, APIs, tests, docs, and security boundaries
skills.orchestration: MUST run before any code modification — gates the execution of all downstream skills

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture design when a breaking API change is detected
Mandatory security review when a change touches auth, session, or data access boundaries

skills.name: event-router
skills.description: Routes system events to registered skill handlers; maintains event-to-skill dispatch map; deduplicates events
skills.orchestration: Sits on the event bus — every system event passes through before reaching skill executors

feedback_routes.description: Alert orchestrator when >10 unhandled events occur in one session

skills.name: code-generator
skills.description: Generates implementation files, scaffolding, and interface stubs from architecture modules, feature plans, or interface contracts
skills.orchestration: Runs after feature-planning; output always routed to clean-code-review and test-generator

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture design when >30% of imports cannot be resolved from the architecture

skills.name: adr-generator
skills.description: Produces immutable Architecture Decision Records from architectural choices, tradeoffs, and alternatives
skills.orchestration: Invoked automatically by architecture-design after every major decision; on-demand for any significant technical choice

skills.name: test-generator
skills.description: Generates unit, integration, e2e, property-based, contract, and parameterized test suites with BDD naming, AAA structure, test data factories, and mutation gap filling
skills.orchestration: Runs after code-generator and after testing-strategy v2.0.0; re-invoked in from_mutation_gaps mode when mutation-test-generator emits backpropagate feedback

feedback_routes.description: Alert testing-strategy when generated coverage estimate is below the defined target
Warn mutation-test-generator when assertion gaps remain unaddressed after from_mutation_gaps invocation

skills.name: code-repair
skills.description: Diagnoses and applies minimal, validated fixes to failing tests, compile errors, type errors, and lint violations
skills.orchestration: Triggered by test.failed events and broken build signals; output routed back through clean-code-review

feedback_routes.description: Route to clean-code-review when all repair candidates fail for human-level refactoring
Mandatory security review when repair touches an auth or data access boundary

skills.name: rollback-manager
skills.description: Safely reverts system state to a prior snapshot on deployment failure, critical pipeline error, or unrecoverable code change
skills.orchestration: Last-resort recovery — triggered on critical pipeline failures, deployment failures, and unrecoverable code errors

feedback_routes.description: Notify deployment-strategy to update environment state after a deployment rollback

skills.name: frontend-ux-architect
skills.description: Design and validate screen structure, navigation, component contracts, accessibility, motion design specs, visual excellence targets, and creative layout strategies for any UI feature; v2.0.0 adds creativity_level, motion_requirements inputs and visual_excellence_targets, motion_spec, creative_recommendations outputs
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2b-design in parallel with database-architect after architecture-design completes

feedback_routes.description: Return to requirement-analyzer when UI requirements are too vague to produce a screen inventory

skills.name: database-architect
skills.description: Design normalized schemas, ERDs, indexing strategies, audit logging, soft-delete patterns, and migration plans; v1.1.0 adds domain_constraints input for multi-tenant schema strategy (shared_db RLS, schema_per_tenant, db_per_tenant) from saas-enterprise-architect
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2b-design in parallel with frontend-ux-architect after architecture-design completes

feedback_routes.description: Return to requirement-analyzer when entity definitions or relationships are too ambiguous to normalize

skills.name: implementation-completeness-auditor
skills.description: Cross-check all requirements against delivered code, tests, UI screens, DB entities, and documentation; produce a readiness score (0–100) and traceability matrix
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-8b-audit after code generation and guard checks complete; output feeds implementation-completeness-guard

feedback_routes.description: Route back to code-generator when a critical functional requirement has no implementation artifact

skills.name: database-guard
skills.description: Enforce database schema safety by blocking destructive migrations, missing FK indexes, unannotated PII, and missing cascade rules
skills.orchestration: Runs as a validation_check gate in phase-7b-guards in parallel with performance-guard and ui-ux-compliance-guard; block verdict halts pipeline

skills.name: performance-guard
skills.description: Detect N+1 query patterns, missing query indexes, bulk operation anti-patterns, and blocking calls before they reach production
skills.orchestration: Runs as a validation_check gate in phase-7b-guards in parallel with database-guard and ui-ux-compliance-guard; block verdict halts pipeline

skills.name: ui-ux-compliance-guard
skills.description: Block hardcoded design values, missing component states, prop contract violations, and accessibility issues; v2.0.0 adds visual_quality_score, ux_quality_score, and motion_quality_score dimensional outputs with creativity_level-aware pass/block thresholds
skills.orchestration: Runs as a validation_check gate in phase-7b-guards in parallel with database-guard and performance-guard; block verdict halts pipeline

skills.name: implementation-completeness-guard
skills.description: Enforce the release readiness threshold by blocking pipeline advancement when the completeness audit score is below the configured minimum
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-8c-release-guard after implementation-completeness-auditor; block verdict is non-bypassable and halts all deployment advancement

skills.name: design-system-generator
skills.description: Generate design token files (including motion, elevation, glass tokens), multi-theme system (light/dark/high-contrast/accessibility), component stubs, and Storybook config from a UX architecture spec; v2.0.0 adds motion_tokens, visual_excellence_targets, and theme_modes support
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2c-design-system after frontend-ux-architect and before code-generator in website pipelines

feedback_routes.description: Return to frontend-ux-architect when a required token category cannot be resolved from the provided token_requirements

skills.name: seo-optimizer
skills.description: Generate sitemap, robots.txt, JSON-LD structured data schemas, Open Graph meta tag specifications, and Core Web Vitals performance budget for any public-facing website
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-8b-seo after code-generator and before deployment-strategy in website pipelines

feedback_routes.description: Return to frontend-ux-architect when the screen inventory has conflicting or ambiguous route definitions that prevent correct sitemap generation

skills.name: prompt-normalizer
skills.description: Pre-routing intent extraction skill that normalizes vague user prompts into structured routing-ready prompts, classifies application domain, and generates a single targeted clarification question
skills.orchestration: Invoked by orchestrator as step 0, before routing table lookup; domain_classification forwarded to all pipeline skills

skills.name: security-guard
skills.description: Converts security-review findings into a binary pass/block pipeline gate with CVSS threshold enforcement, compliance framework scaling, and domain-specific block conditions
skills.orchestration: Runs as a validation_check gate in phase-7b-guards in parallel with database-guard, performance-guard, and ui-ux-compliance-guard; block verdict halts pipeline

feedback_routes.description: Re-run security-review after remediation when blocking findings are present
Re-run architecture-design when OWASP A01 (Broken Access Control) findings are structural
Surface missing security requirements when OWASP categories have no corresponding requirement

skills.name: ci-pipeline-generator
skills.description: Generates executable CI/CD pipeline files (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI YAML, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, .env.example, Kubernetes manifests) from deployment-strategy output
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-9a-ci-scaffold after deployment-strategy and all guard gates have passed; output artifacts written to disk by builder agent

feedback_routes.description: Re-run deployment-strategy when secret references cannot be resolved from the deployment plan
Re-run architecture-design when technology decisions are too vague for pipeline generation

skills.name: ai-agent-specialist
skills.description: Domain specialist that injects AI/agent-specific architecture patterns, safety controls, evaluation frameworks, and implementation standards when building LLM-powered applications, RAG pipelines, or multi-agent systems
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2c-domain-specialist in parallel with architecture-design second pass; activated only when domain_context.domain_primary == 'ai_agent'

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate to requirement-analyzer when requirements are too vague to determine the agent system type

skills.name: mobile-platform-specialist
skills.description: Domain specialist that injects mobile-platform-specific architecture patterns, technology stack selection (iOS/Android/RN/Flutter/MAUI), offline-first architecture, push notification design, App Store compliance requirements, mobile security controls (certificate pinning, Keychain/Keystore), device matrix, and testing strategy when building native or cross-platform mobile apps
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2c-domain-specialist in parallel with architecture-design second pass; activated only when domain_context.domain_primary == 'mobile'

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate to requirement-analyzer when technology stack signals are contradictory and cannot be resolved

skills.name: saas-enterprise-architect
skills.description: Domain specialist that injects SaaS-specific multi-tenancy model selection (shared_db/schema_per_tenant/db_per_tenant), RBAC architecture, subscription billing design (Stripe/Chargebee), enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC/SCIM), compliance framework controls (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS, FedRAMP), white-labeling, and audit logging requirements when building a SaaS platform or B2B enterprise product
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2c-domain-specialist in parallel with architecture-design second pass; activated when domain_context.domain_primary == 'saas' OR 'enterprise'

feedback_routes.description: Surface compliance framework signals detected in requirements when no frameworks are explicitly declared
Backpropagate when usage_based pricing_model is declared but no metering event requirements are defined

skills.name: systems-specialist
skills.description: Domain specialist that injects embedded/IoT/real-time architecture patterns (bare-metal superloop, RTOS task model, IoT edge/cloud), memory budget, HAL design, communication protocol selection (MQTT/CoAP/BLE/LoRaWAN/CAN), OTA firmware update architecture (dual-bank, rollback, signed), safety standard controls (IEC 62443, ISO 26262, IEC 61508), and game development architecture (ECS, game loop, multiplayer netcode) when building embedded systems, IoT platforms, or games
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-2c-domain-specialist in parallel with architecture-design second pass; activated when domain_context.domain_primary == 'embedded_iot' OR 'game'

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate when real_time_class=hard is declared but no timing deadline requirements are defined
Surface safety signals detected in requirements when no safety standard is explicitly declared

skills.name: behavioral-telemetry-collector
skills.description: Collects anonymized, PII-scrubbed behavioral events from the skill pipeline. First check is always opt-out flag — exits immediately if opted out. Ring-buffer storage of up to 500 events per session. Supports capability_gap event type (FEATURE-001).
skills.orchestration: Async fire-and-forget — invoked by orchestrator at skill.completed, skill.failed, gate.passed, and gate.blocked events; never blocks pipeline progression

skills.name: session-insights
skills.description: Aggregates behavioral telemetry events into per-skill performance metrics: invocation counts, success/failure rates, HITL rejection ratios, p95 latency. Writes session_summary to state. Includes gap metrics (total_capability_gaps, top_gap_domains, gap_ids) — FEATURE-001. Read-only on events array.
skills.orchestration: Async — invoked once at pipeline.ended after behavioral-telemetry-collector events are complete; does not block pipeline

feedback_routes.description: Emit warning feedback when any skill has failure_rate > 0.30 or HITL rejection_ratio > 0.30

skills.name: enhancement-dashboard
skills.description: Read-only dashboard renderer. Converts the session_summary from session-insights into a structured Markdown + JSON report. Never modifies system state.
skills.orchestration: On-demand or async after session-insights completes; triggered by user request or orchestrator at end of insights-pipeline; never blocks pipeline

skills.name: adaptive-proposal-generator
skills.description: Analyzes session_summary from session-insights (SKL-048) to generate ranked AdaptationProposal objects covering new skills, skill modifications, new pipeline templates, and agent changes. All proposals require HITL approval — nothing is applied automatically.
skills.orchestration: Invoked at session end after session-insights completes; async, non-blocking. Presents proposals to HITL gate before routing to adaptation-applicator.

skills.name: adaptation-applicator
skills.description: Applies a single HITL-approved AdaptationProposal to the live skill system. Enforces hitl_status == 'approved' as first unconditional check. Creates rollback checkpoint before any write. Runs validate-skills.sh + npm run build after every change. Triggers doc-maintainer on success.
skills.orchestration: Invoked by orchestrator only after HITL gate approves a proposal. Blocking — waits for validate-skills.sh and npm run build to complete before returning.

feedback_routes.description: Emit backpropagate feedback with error details and rollback confirmation when any validation step fails

skills.name: motion-design-architect
skills.description: Design complete motion system — token hierarchy, micro-interactions, page transitions, scroll experiences, choreography, and 3D integration specs
skills.orchestration: sequential

feedback_routes.condition: motion_spec is incomplete or missing required categories
feedback_routes.description: Request expanded motion_spec from frontend-ux-architect

skills.name: ux-research-synthesizer
skills.description: Analyze user flows, map journeys, detect friction, evaluate usability heuristics, audit accessibility experience, and score UX health
skills.orchestration: sequential

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate critical UX findings to trigger redesign of worst-scoring flows

skills.name: creative-experience-architect
skills.description: Architect world-class interfaces — innovative layouts, story-driven flows, immersive backgrounds, premium component enhancements, creative briefs
skills.orchestration: sequential

feedback_routes.condition: creative_brief approved and requires UX architecture updates
feedback_routes.description: Feed approved creative patterns as creative_recommendations back to frontend-ux-architect

skills.name: defect-manager
skills.description: Manages the full defect lifecycle from intake to closure. Creates BUG records and generates companion task chains (INVESTIGATION, FIX, TEST, REVIEW, VALIDATION, CLOSURE) for all reported defects. Integrates with code-repair for FIX execution.
skills.orchestration: sequential

feedback_routes.description: FIX task routed to code-repair for implementation

skills.name: change-request-manager
skills.description: Manages the change request lifecycle from intake to execution. Runs impact analysis, enforces HITL approval, and generates task deltas for feature-planning re-invocation. Does not modify existing tasks directly.
skills.orchestration: sequential

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate task delta to feature-planning for re-planning
Backpropagate new requirements for normalization before feature-planning re-plan

skills.name: work-item-exporter
skills.description: Exports work items to Jira Bulk Import JSON, JSON Lines, and Markdown formats. One-way outbound export only. Runs async at pipeline completion. Includes PII scrubbing before any file write.
skills.orchestration: async

skills.name: work-item-lifecycle-guard
skills.description: Validates work item lifecycle state transitions against the rules in docs/work-item-foundation.md. Returns allow/warn/block verdict. Initial deployment mode: warning. Guard Layer 2 — runs as validation_check before every work item state write.
skills.orchestration: validation_check

skills.name: gap-to-skill-pipeline
skills.description: Reactive pipeline converting a logged capability gap into a registered draft skill. Restores gap_context, pre-populates skill-authoring scaffold (gap_seed mode), runs quality scoring, and requires non-bypassable HITL approval before registration. Entry skill for the gap-to-skill pipeline template.
skills.orchestration: Entry skill of gap-to-skill pipeline; user-initiated or suggested by orchestrator after capability_gap detection

feedback_routes.description: Surface error details to orchestrator for user display

skills.name: semantic-diff-analyzer
skills.description: Compares before/after code to detect behavioral changes — logic shifts, contract changes, security boundary alterations — beyond line-level diffs. Feeds precise impact signals to security-review and test-generator.
skills.orchestration: Invoked by orchestrator after change-impact-analyzer on any code.changed event; runs before security-review and test-generator when analysis_depth != 'quick'

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory security review when auth, session, or data access logic is altered
Re-generate tests when function signatures, preconditions, or return contracts change

skills.name: acceptance-criteria-generator
skills.description: Transforms structured requirements into formal BDD-style Given/When/Then acceptance criteria with AC IDs, negative scenarios, and testability ratings. Creates traceable bridge between business requirements and test generation.
skills.orchestration: Runs after requirement-analyzer; output passed as first-class input to testing-strategy and test-generator to make requirements-to-tests traceability explicit

feedback_routes.description: Re-run requirement analysis when requirements cannot produce valid acceptance criteria
Alert requirement-analyzer when >30% of requirements are rated low testability

skills.name: technical-debt-tracker
skills.description: Maintains a persistent cross-session technical debt register. Calculates debt score (0–100), tracks debt trend across runs, projects maintenance cost with interest model, and surfaces prioritized remediation backlog.
skills.orchestration: Runs after clean-code-review on every pipeline cycle; operation=record on code.changed events; operation=report on demand or at session end

feedback_routes.description: Trigger immediate code review pass when debt score exceeds 80
Alert orchestrator when debt trend is degrading across 3+ consecutive sessions

skills.name: architecture-evolution-planner
skills.description: Produces a phased, sprint-sized migration plan from current to target architecture using strangler-fig, branch-by-abstraction, and expand-contract patterns. Generates feature-flag plans, backward-compat contracts, and ADR stubs per phase.
skills.orchestration: On-demand — triggered when user requests architecture migration or refactoring plan; HITL gate after output before implementation begins

feedback_routes.description: Re-run architecture design when evolution path contains circular module dependencies
Halt and surface to HITL when zero_downtime constraint cannot be satisfied by any phasing

skills.name: compliance-mapper
skills.description: Maps features and architecture against GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 clauses to produce a compliance traceability matrix, gap report, and audit evidence checklist. Detects features introducing new regulatory obligations.
skills.orchestration: Runs in phase-7c-compliance after security-review when compliance_frameworks are specified in requirements; non-blocking unless critical gaps found

feedback_routes.description: Add missing compliance requirement when a critical regulatory clause has no implementing feature
Alert orchestrator for HITL when features introduce undeclared regulatory obligations

skills.name: api-deprecation-manager
skills.description: Manages the full API version lifecycle (current → deprecated → sunset). Auto-generates migration guides from breaking changes, creates consumer migration work items, and enforces sunset timelines with violation detection.
skills.orchestration: Triggered on any breaking API change detected by change-impact-analyzer; also invoked on-demand for version lifecycle management

feedback_routes.description: Alert when a breaking change is introduced without a corresponding deprecation notice
Alert orchestrator when an API has passed its sunset date and is still active

skills.name: infrastructure-cost-estimator
skills.description: Projects monthly cloud infrastructure costs (AWS/GCP/Azure) from deployment-strategy output. Produces per-provider breakdowns, 3 alternative configs, cost optimization suggestions, and cost-per-feature attribution.
skills.orchestration: Runs after deployment-strategy and before the deploy HITL gate; output presented alongside deployment_approval_request

feedback_routes.description: Backpropagate to deployment-strategy to optimize resource selection when cost exceeds declared budget
Surface to HITL when one provider is >50% cheaper for provider selection decision

skills.name: mutation-test-generator
skills.description: Performs static mutation analysis: generates code mutations (flipped conditions, removed null checks, changed operators), infers kill status by analyzing test assertions, and produces mutation score + assertion gaps to eliminate surviving mutants.
skills.orchestration: Runs after test-generator as an optional quality enhancement; triggered on-demand or when coverage is high but mutation confidence is unknown

feedback_routes.description: Pass assertion_gaps to test-generator for targeted test strengthening when mutation score < 60%
Alert clean-code-review when critical functions have high mutation survival rates

skills.name: skill-simulator
skills.description: Runs a complete pipeline in simulation mode — all skills execute, outputs generated in preview form, no disk writes — and produces a unified preview report: files that would change, HITL gates predicted, block risks, token estimate, and go/no-go summary.
skills.orchestration: Invoked before any full pipeline run when user requests preview; also triggered automatically on first run of a new pipeline configuration

feedback_routes.description: Surface to HITL before real execution when simulation detects critical block risks

skills.name: multi-repo-coordinator
skills.description: Coordinates changes across multiple repositories. Maintains cross-repo dependency registry, detects breaking changes that ripple across service boundaries, generates synchronized work items per affected repo, and produces migration coordination plans.
skills.orchestration: Invoked when primary_repo.type == 'library' or breaking API changes detected; requires register operations before analyze-impact is useful

feedback_routes.description: Alert orchestrator for HITL when circular cross-repo dependencies are found
Mandatory HITL when 3+ repos are affected by a single change

skills.name: domain-knowledge-extractor
skills.description: Extracts reusable domain patterns (entities, security, APIs, compliance) from completed pipeline artifacts into a persistent knowledge base. On subsequent pipelines in the same domain, injects relevant patterns as context to reduce clarification rounds and surface domain risks earlier.
skills.orchestration: extract runs async at pipeline.ended (non-blocking); inject runs at pipeline.started when domain knowledge exists for detected domain

feedback_routes.description: Inform orchestrator of new domain for future tracking and knowledge accumulation
Trigger context compression when a domain knowledge base grows beyond token budget

skills.name: pipeline-branching
skills.description: Forks pipeline at architecture-design into two named branches (A/B), runs each through planning and quality skills in parallel, produces a side-by-side comparison scorecard with a recommendation, and enforces a mandatory HITL gate for branch selection before promoting winning artifacts.
skills.orchestration: Triggered explicitly when user requests parallel architecture exploration; not part of default pipeline; requires both architecture variants as input

feedback_routes.description: Escalate to HITL with additional criteria when both branches score within 5% of each other
Report failed branch and fall back to single-branch execution

skills.name: data-pipeline-architect
skills.description: Use when designing production-grade data pipeline architectures for batch ETL, streaming, or lakehouse patterns. Triggers on: "design a data pipeline", "ETL architecture", "streaming pipeline design", "data lakehouse", "Kafka pipeline". Do NOT use for single-table queries or simple CRUD APIs — use database-architect for pure schema work instead.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when domain == 'data' or requirements include ETL/streaming processing

feedback_routes.description: HITL when source SLAs conflict with pipeline latency requirements

skills.name: data-quality-validator
skills.description: Use when defining or enforcing data quality rules at pipeline stages. Triggers on: "data quality rules", "dbt tests", "Great Expectations", "data freshness", "data SLA enforcement". Do NOT use for schema design — use database-architect. Do NOT use without a pipeline architecture output to validate against.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after data-pipeline-architect; runs at each pipeline stage boundary to validate schema conformance

feedback_routes.description: HITL when critical fields fail quality checks in production

skills.name: ml-pipeline-architect
skills.description: Use when designing machine learning pipelines including feature stores, training orchestration, model serving, and monitoring. Triggers on: "ML pipeline", "feature store design", "model training architecture", "MLflow", "model serving". Do NOT use for pure data ETL without ML components — use data-pipeline-architect instead.
skills.orchestration: Invoked when requirements include ML model training, feature engineering, or model serving endpoints

feedback_routes.description: HITL when estimated training infrastructure cost exceeds configured budget threshold

skills.name: analytics-schema-designer
skills.description: Use when designing data warehouse schemas, dimensional models, or BI semantic layers for analytical workloads. Triggers on: "dimensional model", "star schema", "data warehouse design", "dbt models", "BI semantic layer". Do NOT use for transactional OLTP schemas — use database-architect instead.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after data-pipeline-architect when output includes analytical/reporting requirements; runs before code-generator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when business question definitions produce ambiguous fact table granularity

skills.name: data-contract-enforcer
skills.description: Use when establishing or enforcing contracts between data producers and consumers across pipeline boundaries. Triggers on: "data contract", "schema contract", "breaking schema change", "producer-consumer agreement", "data SLA contract". Do NOT use without defined producer schemas and consumer requirements.
skills.orchestration: Invoked at pipeline.schema_changed events or explicitly when defining cross-team data interfaces; blocks deployment on breaking changes

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory HITL when a breaking schema change affects registered consumers

skills.name: api-design-architect
skills.description: Use when designing REST APIs, producing OpenAPI specifications, or defining API versioning and rate limiting strategies. Triggers on: "design a REST API", "OpenAPI spec", "API versioning", "rate limiting design", "API contract". Do NOT use for GraphQL APIs — use graphql-architect. Do NOT use for event-driven messaging — use event-schema-designer.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when modules expose HTTP/REST interfaces; produces OpenAPI spec consumed by code-generator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when proposed changes break existing API contracts with registered consumers

skills.name: graphql-architect
skills.description: Use when designing GraphQL APIs, federation topologies, or resolver architectures. Triggers on: "GraphQL schema", "Apollo Federation", "resolver design", "GraphQL subscription", "schema stitching". Do NOT use for REST APIs — use api-design-architect. Do NOT use for event-driven messaging — use event-schema-designer.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when modules expose GraphQL interfaces; federation_config required when multiple subgraphs present

feedback_routes.description: HITL when subgraph dependency cycle is detected in federation topology

skills.name: event-schema-designer
skills.description: Use when designing event schemas for message queues, event buses, or streaming platforms. Triggers on: "event schema", "AsyncAPI", "Kafka topic design", "schema registry", "event catalog". Do NOT use for REST/GraphQL APIs. Do NOT use without defined service boundaries from microservices-architect.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after microservices-architect defines service boundaries; schema_registry_config blocks deployment until compatibility rules pass

feedback_routes.description: HITL when proposed event schema change breaks backward compatibility with registered consumers

skills.name: microservices-architect
skills.description: Use when decomposing monolithic systems into microservices or validating service boundaries using DDD. Triggers on: "decompose into microservices", "service boundary design", "bounded context map", "DDD decomposition", "Conway's Law analysis". Do NOT use for high-level architecture — run architecture-design first. Do NOT use for GraphQL federation topology — use graphql-architect.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when system has 3+ bounded contexts or team asks about service decomposition

feedback_routes.description: HITL when two equally valid decompositions exist — requires domain expert input

skills.name: event-sourcing-designer
skills.description: Use when designing CQRS/event-sourcing architectures including aggregates, event stores, and projections. Triggers on: "event sourcing", "CQRS", "aggregate design", "event store", "projection design". Do NOT use for simple CRUD systems. Do NOT use without service boundaries from microservices-architect.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after microservices-architect when aggregates require full audit trail or temporal queries; feeds database-architect for event store schema

feedback_routes.description: HITL when aggregate boundary enforcement conflicts with query projection requirements

skills.name: distributed-resilience-architect
skills.description: Use when designing resilience patterns for distributed systems including circuit breakers, retries, bulkheads, and saga compensation. Triggers on: "circuit breaker design", "resilience patterns", "timeout hierarchy", "retry policy", "bulkhead pattern". Do NOT use for single-service applications. Do NOT use before microservices boundaries are defined.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after microservices-architect; fmea_report is consumed by chaos-engineering-designer for experiment design

feedback_routes.description: HITL when compensation transaction semantics conflict with business consistency requirements

skills.name: cloud-architecture-reviewer
skills.description: Use when reviewing cloud architectures against AWS/GCP/Azure Well-Architected Framework pillars. Triggers on: "Well-Architected review", "cloud architecture review", "AWS WAR", "cloud cost optimization", "cloud security gaps". Do NOT use for on-premises-only systems. Do NOT use as a substitute for architecture-design.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design for any cloud-hosted system; can also run standalone for existing architecture reviews

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory HITL when a critical security pillar gap is identified in the architecture

skills.name: serverless-architect
skills.description: Use when designing serverless function architectures with Lambda/Cloud Functions/Azure Functions. Triggers on: "serverless architecture", "Lambda design", "Cloud Functions", "event-driven serverless", "cold start mitigation". Do NOT use when compute requirements need persistent connections or long-running processes.
skills.orchestration: Invoked when architecture includes event-driven or workload-spiky compute requirements; cost_estimate feeds infrastructure-cost-estimator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when provisioned concurrency cost required to meet cold-start SLA exceeds serverless cost benefit

skills.name: container-orchestration-architect
skills.description: Use when designing Kubernetes cluster topologies, Helm charts, autoscaling policies, or GitOps workflows. Triggers on: "Kubernetes design", "Helm chart structure", "cluster topology", "GitOps pipeline", "pod autoscaling". Do NOT use for serverless workloads — use serverless-architect. Do NOT use for VM-based deployments.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when deployment target is Kubernetes; secrets-management-architect should run first for vault integration

feedback_routes.description: HITL when workload resource requirements exceed cluster capacity model

skills.name: threat-model-designer
skills.description: Use when producing structured threat models, STRIDE analyses, or MITRE ATT&CK mappings from architecture diagrams. Triggers on: "threat model", "STRIDE analysis", "attack surface map", "trust boundary", "MITRE ATT&CK mapping". Do NOT use as a substitute for security-review — threat modeling informs security review, not replaces it.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design and before security-review; risk_matrix feeds security-guard severity thresholds

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory HITL when a CRITICAL-rated threat with no existing mitigation is identified

skills.name: secrets-management-architect
skills.description: Use when designing secrets management systems, Vault architectures, or credential rotation policies. Triggers on: "secrets management", "Vault architecture", "secret rotation", "PKI design", "dynamic secrets". Do NOT use for general security review — use security-review for that. Do NOT design secrets management without a known deployment target.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design; injection_patterns consumed by container-orchestration-architect for Vault sidecar/CSI driver config

feedback_routes.description: HITL when inventory reveals secrets stored in environment variables or config files in production

skills.name: devsecops-pipeline-designer
skills.description: Use when embedding security scanning stages into CI/CD pipelines including SAST, DAST, SCA, and SBOM generation. Triggers on: "DevSecOps pipeline", "SAST integration", "container scanning", "SBOM generation", "SLSA compliance". Do NOT use before security-review has run — this skill implements findings, not discovers them.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after security-review; pipeline_stages consumed by ci-pipeline-generator to produce executable YAML with security gates

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory HITL when CRITICAL CVE is detected in base image or direct dependency

skills.name: load-test-designer
skills.description: Use when designing load test scenarios, defining SLO-based thresholds, or integrating performance tests into CI pipelines. Triggers on: "load test design", "performance test scenarios", "k6 scripts", "Gatling test", "soak test". Do NOT use for unit or integration tests — use testing-strategy for that.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after testing-strategy when performance/load requirements are present; slo_targets consumed from slo-sla-designer output

feedback_routes.description: HITL when baseline load test reveals existing SLO violation before new feature deployment

skills.name: profiling-advisor
skills.description: Use when analyzing profiling data — CPU flamegraphs, heap dumps, query execution plans — to identify performance bottlenecks. Triggers on: "profile analysis", "CPU hotspot", "heap dump analysis", "query plan optimization", "GC pressure". Do NOT use without actual profiling data — generate a load test with load-test-designer first.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after load-test-designer generates profiling artifacts; optimization_backlog feeds performance-guard regression gate

feedback_routes.description: Immediate HITL when heap profile shows unbounded memory growth pattern

skills.name: slo-sla-designer
skills.description: Use when defining SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, SLA commitments, or reliability roadmaps for production services. Triggers on: "SLO design", "SLI definition", "error budget", "SLA commitments", "reliability targets". Do NOT use for infrastructure sizing — use cloud-architecture-reviewer. Do NOT define SLOs before user journeys are understood.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design; slo_targets consumed by load-test-designer, observability, and runbook-generator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when SLO targets for dependent services have incompatible error budgets

skills.name: runbook-generator
skills.description: Use when generating operational runbooks linked to alert policies, SLOs, and service dependencies. Triggers on: "generate runbooks", "operational runbook", "incident response procedures", "escalation tree", "on-call runbook". Do NOT use without defined alerts and SLO targets.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after slo-sla-designer and observability; runbook_url annotations fed back to alert definitions in observability

feedback_routes.description: HITL when escalation tree references teams or contacts not in the organizational directory

skills.name: chaos-engineering-designer
skills.description: Use when designing chaos engineering experiments to validate system resilience. Triggers on: "chaos engineering", "fault injection", "resilience experiments", "chaos testing", "blast radius analysis". Do NOT use when distributed-resilience-architect has not yet produced a resilience baseline — run that skill first.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after distributed-resilience-architect; requires fmea_report as input; scheduling_plan feeds CI/CD chaos gate when continuous_chaos=true

feedback_routes.description: Mandatory HITL when a running experiment propagates beyond the configured blast_radius_tolerance

skills.name: state-management-architect
skills.description: Use when designing frontend or mobile state management architecture including library selection and pattern recommendations. Triggers on: "state management design", "Redux vs Zustand", "client state architecture", "server state caching", "offline state". Do NOT use for backend state — use distributed-resilience-architect for distributed state.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design for frontend/mobile apps; library_recommendations consumed by code-generator for store scaffolding

feedback_routes.description: HITL when server state and client state boundaries conflict in component design

skills.name: micro-frontend-architect
skills.description: Use when designing micro-frontend architectures, Module Federation configurations, or multi-team frontend composition strategies. Triggers on: "micro-frontend architecture", "Module Federation", "single-spa", "shell app design", "frontend composition". Do NOT use for monolithic SPAs — use architecture-design or frontend-ux-architect.
skills.orchestration: Invoked when team_count > 2 or explicit multi-team frontend ownership is required; module_federation_config feeds code-generator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when multiple MFEs declare conflicting singleton library versions (React, router)

skills.name: web-performance-optimizer
skills.description: Use when optimizing web application performance for Core Web Vitals, bundle size, or rendering strategy. Triggers on: "Core Web Vitals", "bundle optimization", "LCP optimization", "rendering mode selection", "image optimization". Do NOT use for backend API performance — use profiling-advisor for that.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design for web apps; optimization_plan feeds CI lighthouse budget gates via ci-pipeline-generator

feedback_routes.description: HITL when LCP target cannot be achieved without architectural changes (SSR/ISR migration)

skills.name: ddd-architect
skills.description: Use when applying Domain-Driven Design to produce bounded context maps, ubiquitous language glossaries, or aggregate designs. Triggers on: "domain-driven design", "bounded context map", "ubiquitous language", "aggregate design", "DDD strategic patterns". Do NOT use as a substitute for architecture-design — DDD enriches domain understanding before architectural decomposition.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after requirement-analyzer when domain complexity warrants DDD; bounded_context_map consumed by microservices-architect

feedback_routes.description: HITL when two candidate bounded contexts have identical ubiquitous language — requires domain expert clarification

skills.name: caching-strategy-designer
skills.description: Use when designing caching strategies for services including Redis configurations, HTTP cache headers, CDN rules, and invalidation patterns. Triggers on: "caching strategy", "Redis design", "cache invalidation", "CDN caching rules", "cache stampede". Do NOT use for database query optimization — use database-architect for indexes.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when performance requirements include sub-millisecond reads or high traffic patterns

feedback_routes.description: HITL when strong consistency requirements conflict with cache TTL settings for critical data

skills.name: localization-architect
skills.description: Use when designing internationalization or localization architectures for multi-locale applications. Triggers on: "i18n architecture", "localization design", "RTL support", "ICU message format", "translation pipeline". Do NOT use for single-locale applications. Do NOT configure translation tools without locale file structure in place.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when target_locales.length > 1; translation_pipeline config consumed by ci-pipeline-generator for validation

feedback_routes.description: HITL when RTL layout requirements conflict with existing design system component grid assumptions

skills.name: product-analytics-designer
skills.description: Use when designing product analytics systems including event taxonomy, funnel definitions, or A/B testing frameworks. Triggers on: "product analytics design", "event taxonomy", "funnel definition", "A/B experiment framework", "analytics data model". Do NOT use for infrastructure metrics — use observability for that.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after feature-planning when product_goals include growth, retention, or conversion metrics; analytics_data_model feeds data-pipeline-architect

feedback_routes.description: HITL when proposed event tracking conflicts with GDPR/CCPA consent requirements

skills.name: realtime-system-architect
skills.description: Use when designing real-time systems with WebSockets, Server-Sent Events, or WebRTC. Triggers on: "real-time architecture", "WebSocket design", "WebRTC", "collaborative editing", "CRDT", "presence system". Do NOT use for batch or polling-based systems. Do NOT use for simple REST APIs.
skills.orchestration: Invoked after architecture-design when use cases include real-time collaboration, live feeds, or bidirectional streaming

feedback_routes.description: HITL when connection scale requires multi-region WebSocket routing with sticky sessions
